records.ety: [F. immdiat. See In- not, and Mediate.]

defs: Not separated in respect to place by anything intervening; proximate; close; as, immediate contact.
quotes: You are the most immediate to our throne.

defs: Not deferred by an interval of time; present; instant.
quotes: Death . . . not yet inflicted, as he feared, By some immediate stroke.

defs: Acting with nothing interposed or between, or without the intervention of another object as a cause, means, or agency; acting, perceived, or produced, directly; as, an immediate cause.
quotes: The immediate knowledge of the past is therefore impossible.
